sentences.text: Hey, how are you?
Hi, I'm good, how are you?
I'm doing well!
I'm doing well.
Thanks for hopping on with me today.
Absolutely!
Do you have a good, labor Day weekend?
I did, I did, how about you?
Yeah, it was, it was very nice and relaxing, can't complain.
Good, that's awesome.
Happy Tuesday!
Yeah, thanks, you too, we're on a shortened week, so that's always a plus.
Yes, exactly.
Hopefully it goes by fast.
You and me both.
Did you, did you do anything fun for Labor Day?
My boyfriend's family is from Wilmington, North Carolina, so we went down there and had some beach time and hung out with his family.
How about you?
That sounds exciting.
Yeah, my, my wife is from Franklin, Tennessee.
I'm in Nashville, but, we were there, we hung out, we grilled out, And then we watched college football.
Yeah, it's back.
Finally.
Are you a big football fan?
Not big, but I keep up with it.
I went to Auburn, so I try to keep up with… their team, and then here in Charlottesville, which is where I'm based, UVA is… Keeps us somewhat entertained.
For the most part.
So, are you from Virginia, or are you from, like, Alabama?
Yeah, I'm from Virginia, I'm from Charlottesville.
And what made you choose Auburn?
Just wanted to go south.
I had some family that went there, and…
Okay.
just… I just liked it.
No real reason, honestly.
Fair, fair.
I've heard good things about Auburn and their campus and, like, the, I guess the student life there, so…
Yeah, yeah, it was great.
It was… it was a great experience.
For sure.
I, I missed out on the D1 experience.
I played college basketball and college baseball, but I played for a D3 school.
Oh, nice.
But yeah, I always… it's like, it was the one thing, when I was graduating, I was like, okay, do I go play college ball, or do I…
Yeah…
stop playing the sports that I love, and go experience the D1 atmosphere, and I chose to keep playing sports, so…
Well, can't do it wrong.
Yeah, yeah, it was a win-win either way, so… But, cool.
I, I know you're super busy, so I won't keep you all day.

So, I guess the way I envision our call going today is I had a couple of questions for you, just about your portfolio and what you were seeing, and then I'm sure you wanted to know more about us and what we had to offer, so I'll explain that to you, and then we can kind of see where to go from there.
Perfect.
Sweet.
And I tried doing as much research as possible, just so I didn't have to play the 20-question game, but… It looked like you guys have, like, 8 properties now, is that right?
8, yes.
Okay, and do you own and manage all of those?
Yes.
Okay.
Sweet.
With those 8 properties, what are you doing advertising-wise right now?
So we have, you know, Zillow, Apartments.

com, ApartmentList, our 3 main ILSs that we use, and then we work with RESPage, who does our, like, PPC, and Google Ads, and all of that, and then, social media, somewhat, a little bit, that's kind of a work in progress.
And then, depending on the market, we'll do different things, so some of the markets will do radio, some will have contracts with, like, the local news station for targeted emails.
So it kind of gets more a la carte based on the property and the market.
After those big groups.
That makes sense, but your main three are the Zillow Apartments and lists, and then ResPage, obviously, for the Google aspects.
Yeah.
Is social media being handled in-house for you?
Yeah, RestPage does a little bit, but for the most part, in-house.
Okay, cool, and then aside from that, it really depends on the property, the market, what it's experiencing, like, if it needs a boost in traffic and everything like that?
Yeah, exactly.
Okay.
Sweet.
Well, that leads me to that next question, is what have you been seeing lately?
How's renter traffic, renter demand, and occupancy?
Across the board, it's good.

We have a few properties, that… have had more dips than usual recently, and then we have, like, our Chester, we're just outside of Richmond Market, struggles with… they get a lot of traffic, but struggles with qualified traffic.
So that's a constant battle there.
But across the board, it's been… it's been good.
Okay.
Gotcha.
So, Chester, you said quality traffic issues.
Just like a lot of applicants, but then they're, like, not answering the phones, or they, like, have, like, a bunch of bad credit issues.
Bad credit, evictions, whatever.
They'll get… a lot of people get denied up there for…
action.
various reasons.
Yeah, yeah, it's kind of all over the place.
I gotcha, okay.
Well, perfect.
That, are you using AppFolio for all of your properties?
Yeah, we actually just transitioned to Appfolio.
We were on Yardi for a long time, and just transitioned to Appfolio in March.
Okay.
What, what caused the transition?
Really, like, user-friendliness of it all, and, like, reporting for our backend.
Okay.
So, overall, it's been a good switch.
Their marketing integrations with, like.
things like Apartments.
com and Zillow aren't great.
But… I guess they're working on it.
Yeah, yeah, they're… they're trying to improve that for sure.
We fully integrate with them, and we have for years, so, like, we don't have any issues on that end.
I was… I was curious, honestly, because I've always… I've never heard somebody going from, like, Yardi to Appfolio, but I've heard of them going from Appfolio to Yardi.
Understood.
So I wasn't sure if there was anything in particular that caused it or not.
Yeah, I think, I mean, I… that's not totally my wheelhouse, but I think the bottom line was Yardi was just becoming very dated, and they weren't keeping up with The trends either, so… I'm not sure.
I think our finance department really led the charge on that one, and then, I know from a residence standpoint, their app and interface is much, much better with that folio, so…
Very simplistic with that folio.
It's hard to mess something up there.
Yeah, exactly.
Sweet.
So, I am… I was also looking at your website… sorry, I've got your website pulled up over here as well.
It looks like you're adding another property in 2026, and 3 in the pipeline for 2027 as well?
Yeah, those, I mean, those dates are a little bit fluid, but yes, we've got… We've got, yeah, 4 in the pipeline.
Gotcha.
Nice.
That's, that's a lot of work, but.
We're growing fast, and we're a small but mighty team.
It's a good problem to have, though.
Yes, exactly.
Good.
So, cool.
Well, that's, that's the big questions I have.
Thank you for answering those.
I'll kind of dive in for you, explain to you what Rentable is, what we do, and we can see where to go from there.
Perfect.
So, I'll share my screen here.
Can you see the so-col?
Hang on, it did not share.
Yes, I can see it.
Okay, you should see AI Marketing for Multifamily?
Yep.
Okay, cool.
So, in terms of Rentable, what we do, who we are, first and foremost, we're an internet listing site, just like your Zillows, Apartments.
com's apartment lists.
We do differ in a couple of ways from all of those sites.
One would be the fact that we have a massive syndicate network, and the other one is the fact that we're super cost-effective.
When I say that, I mean, like, we're pennies to the dollar compared to your Apartments.
coms and Zillow's.
But in terms of what we offer, for our rentable ILS.
So, we're integrating with that folio, so anytime you're updating information on your end, pricing, availability, you name it, it'll pull and it'll reflect in our dashboard and our network.
From our site, we're gonna syndicate it out to all of our syndicate network.
When it comes down to our syndicate network.
it's the largest in the industry.
We go literally everywhere except for Apartments.
com.
And then what we do is we utilize an AI tool to make sure we're not duplicating your listings on any other sites.
So, for example, you're already on Zillow portfolio-wide.
we wouldn't push there and duplicate any listings.
Same thing with list.
But, like, if you had… I'm just gonna talk portfolio-wide and pretend like you only had, like, 4 of your properties on apartment list.
We wouldn't push and duplicate there for those 4, but for the 4 that weren't utilizing it, we would still push and get you there.
Does that make sense?
Yeah.
Okay, cool.
So, our real focus here is filling in the gaps, getting your property's maximum exposure, and just driving traffic from all the different sources that you're not currently utilizing.
Bye.
On top of that, we have an all-inclusive targeted social ad as well, where we're essentially reaching out to runners versus waiting for them to come to you.
And the way it'll work is anytime somebody's going on a search engine, they're saying, show me apartments in Charlottesville, or show me apartments in Glen Cove.
Next time they get on their Facebook or Instagram feed, within, like, 30, 45 seconds of scrolling, we'll hit them with a targeted rentable ad that looks like this.

And it'll have pictures of your individual property, some details about it, whether it's, like, pet-friendly, what the lowest advertised pricing is, if there's a pool, something to help it stand out and really attract that renter.
And then, where we differ from a lot of other companies that do social ads is usually those companies do one of two things.
They redirect that customer.
If they were to click learn more, they redirect them to their personal site, so then they can see all the available ads that they're… and properties that they're doing this for.
And the other route is they'll redirect them to a property website, which at that point, you kind of lose track of Did this person become a lead?
Did it come from here?
Did it not?
You don't really know.

With ours, all of ours are lead-generating ads, so when they click Learn More, we're gonna have them fill out a form that captures their name, their email, and their phone number, and we'll deliver that to you as a guest card directly into your app folio.
And that's how all of our leads come in.
We can deliver them directly into AppFolio, we can send them to an on-site leasing email address, both, whichever you'd prefer.
So would the lead then be… Like, would the source say Facebook, or what would it say?
Source would say rentable.
So all of our leads will come in, and they'll be branded as a rentable lead.
On the back end, though, we diversify it from rentable, rentable affiliate, or paid social.
Okay, so we'll be able to see that breakdown.
we will be able to see that breakdown.
When you do your monthly calls with us, sometimes the CSM's able to bring that to the call as well, the success manager.
But a lot of times, we just kind of go through the dashboard and everything looks like a rentable lead.
Okay.
Which kind of leads me to the next thing I was going to talk about, because you're quite… like, you had mentioned one of your properties had quality traffic issues.
We can kind of help with that as well.
We can ask a lot more questions, like, the generic questions are name, email, phone number.
We can ask, like, when are you looking to move?
We can ask, what's the bedroom floor plan you're looking for?
We can ask, like, a handful of questions to… to kind of make that a more qualified renter for you, and make sure that they're more serious about wanting to actually live there.
Bye-bye.
The only question I don't think we can ask, though, is, like, do you have an eviction on your… On your record.
Okay, yeah, that's fine.

But yeah, and then the other way we can kind of help with that, too, is that same AI tool that's making sure we're not duplicating listings, it's working in the background as well to make sure that we're getting you on the best performing sites… best performing sites.
So, if we syndicate to 30 sites, we'll say, here's your top 3 sites for this property, here's your bottom 3.

So that way, instead of us trying to get results out of these bottom 3 that just aren't doing anything, we can take the spin that We would use there, and put it towards the top 3 to double down on the efforts.
And how often is that, like, re-evaluated?
Or is it all AI changing it in real time?
It's all AI changing in real time.
Typically, you see a difference, like, over the course of a month, though.
Okay.
And then the other thing it's doing as well is
different companies have different… different goals, right?
Some want more traffic, some want more quality traffic.

So, like, in your case, for… you said the Chester, like, if your goal is to get more traffic, whatnot, we can put the focus towards the highest performing site, but if it's more towards, like, the quality, we can put it towards not just the highest quantity, but the best quality.
Which ones are converting to leases?
Which ones are turning into tours?
Which site's bringing the best?
So we can kind of gear the AI's focus towards whichever one of those two.
Okay.
I'll pause there for a second, because I know I've said a lot.
What questions do you have?
I don't think any, yet.
Okay.
Fair.
The only other thing besides pricing that I have is I have some success stories.
I looked up a couple of companies that we're working with in your area.
One is Touchstone Property Management.
Another one is out of DC, but they have a lot of properties through Virginia.
It's TM Associates.
And then… one is out of Maryland.
But they have a lot of properties throughout the Virginia area as well, Armiger Management.
All of them are averaging less than $4 cost per lead right now.
Okay.
And they've all been on with us for… I wanna say it was, like, 3 plus years when I looked into them.
Okay.
So, we've seen a lot of success, especially in your… your area of the country, But yeah.
I guess, before I… before I show you the price, how does this compare to what you're currently doing now?

I mean, the price is honestly the big thing, where… especially in our Charlottesville market, where we have 3 properties, I mean, the amount of apartment complexes that are currently under construction is crazy, so competition within the next, you know, year or two is really gonna… Be in the front of our minds, so… And I'm currently working on our 2026 budget, which is… one of the reasons I wanted to do this call, to see… we're really just trying to… diversify and expand the… our marketing in general.
So… But the pricing is the big thing, so…
Totally fair, totally fair.
Would you be looking to go portfolio-wide when bringing on a new partner?
It just depends, it just depends.
I think, probably.
Yeah.
Alright, so…
That changed me well, like, if it's an option.

like, beta test it with, like, the Charlottesville properties, and then, like, see how it goes, and then roll it out portfolio-wide, but it just depends on, you know, the ease of it, how much… training is going to be involved, if any, with the property managers, and just everything that comes along with it.
So… I can't really give you a certain fast answer on that one yet.
All good.
I'm gonna pull this up, too, so I can kind of show you the training that we provide.
We do provide training to kind of walk you through our backend dashboard, just to… to see it, but it's very simplistic.

When we started in the industry, our goal was to make it easy for renters to find an apartment to live in, and to make it easier on property management companies, because the last thing they need is more work and… and have to go through 6 different trainings just to figure out how to use the product.
So… Let me… here, I'll show you the price, and then I'll show you that.
Our price is only $1.
99 a month per property.
Okay, no, it doesn't matter how many units you have?
Correct.
It could be a 500-unit complex, it could be a 120-unit complex, it's $199 a month.
And…
And that's, like, you just have one package, there's no, like, pay more, get more.
Correct.
So it gets you everything, it gets you our rental network, our partnerships, our paid social.
The only time I've ever seen somebody pay more is, like, maybe they'll add, like, an additional social boost on the side to go with their property.
So, like, we do social boosts already, like, the social ads, but sometimes companies are like, oh, well, we want to put an additional $100 a month towards just the social ads for that property.
So I've seen people do that, but in terms of, like, a gold, diamond, platinum level, like Apartments.
com does, we don't do that.
Got it.
That's good.
Yeah, it's… you know, the issue with Apartments.

com is They… oh… they have a lot of traffic, but the properties that you see at the front are going to be the ones that are willing to pay the $1,500, $2,000 a month per property, if not more, depending on the market.
And if they don't want to pay that much, then they kind of fall down, and… then they don't get seen as much.
Their prices are absolutely ridiculous.
Yeah, I talked with somebody, they're like, oh no, we're paying, like, $3,000 a month for our property.
Yeah, that's us.
That's what we're doing.
Well, a couple of them.
For a couple.
Can you see this?
Yes.
Okay, so this is… I just pulled up TM Associates because they were the top of mind.
So, when somebody logs in, like, when you were to log… if you were to log in, Ann, you would clock in here, you would see all the properties that we're advertising for.
You can go into analytics here.
And then you can see over the… it presets to the past 30 days.
So over the past 30 days, we've brought TM Associates 1,752 leads.
And then you can go down here, you can see how many were for each property.
So, Apple Tree Gardens got 114 in the past 30 days, 61 of those were emails, 53 were calls.
And then you can go and you can also change it to however long you want.
So, like, if we go back and look at the past 90 days…
We've brought 5,470 leads.
This property, 352, 197 were email, 155 were calls.
So can you then, like, break it down, especially for the emails, like, by name?
Yep, so then that's where I was going next.

So, like, leads here, if you click into leads, you can see who the lead was, what they said, what their email is, and which property it was for, and what day and time it was that they applied, or, like.
became a leader.
Yeah.
And then you can go and you can… you can search it by specific properties.
So if we did, what was the apple tree?
You can actually click here, and then all these leads are solely for Apple Tree.
Interesting.
You can look per property, by property.
You can also go into calls.
So, like, if you go into the calls, you can see this was the number for this property on 8-4, and then you can go in, click play, and listen to what that call was with the leasing agent.
So that allows you to also give, like, some training as well.

If you go on, you listen to a call, and you hear somebody not acting professional, or maybe, like, saying the right thing, or answering a question the correct way, you can kind of give them Feedback and coaching based on that phone call, too.
Yeah.
So, and then we can limit access for everybody, too.

So, like, in this case, they have a lot of people that have access, but, like, for you, we could give you access to seeing the entire portfolio, but then we can give regional managers just access to seeing their region.
Got it.
So, we can kind of divvy it up, too, so…
So what is on the left?
What was the, like, Google reviews and all of that?
Yeah, so I've actually been asked that question.
We have two products, when it comes to our advertising.

We have our rentable ILS with social ads, and then on the other side, we have our Maven AI product, which handles more of, like, Google automation on the properties, like Google Business pages, it handles review management.
Social AI, which is, like, doing up to, I think it's 3 postings a week per property on the property's Facebook page.
Just kind of keeping that activity, and then over time, increasing Google rankings and driving more,
Organic traffic to your properties.
Got it.
So… How do you…
How do you… I lost my train of thought.
Well, another question I have.
At Folio Integration, Have you ever dealt with… two-phased properties.
Yeah, like, where it's Phase 1 finished by now, and then, like, Phase 2 is finishing at the end of September type thing?
No, like…
a property that, for example, one that's been established, it's 20 years old, is two phases, so it has… it was built in two phases, which means it has two separate, like, ownership group.
We own it, but it has two different sets of investors, and… Phase 1… is its own LLC, Phase 2 is its own LLC, but, like, for marketing purposes, they're all one property.
So, however it's set up in Appfolio, if you have it set up as one property in Appfolio for marketing, then we'll pull it as one property.
Well, it's not.
Like, you can't set up… two different… like, two different businesses as one.
So they're… this is what we've run into with Appfolio, and, like, Apartments.
com, is Apartments.
com can't pull both phases to market as one.
Yeah, that would be a… we… so… We can and we cannot.
The reason I say we cannot is because if it's in Appfolio as two properties and marketed as two properties, we would pull it as two properties.
The other way that we could do it for a workaround, though, is we could build the listing manually.
Where we only built it as one property, but we put all the units from both properties as one.
The issue when it came to doing that part, though, is it would have to be manually updated by yourself or by our… our success manager.
Like, if you lease a unit, or if you have one come available, or things like that.
Yeah.
But we have the ability to build it manually, so if you wanted it only as one property, we could technically do that.
But we could also do it to where we advertise it as two properties, but we send all the emails to the same email address, or to the same Appfolio account.
But then we'd be paying $1.
99 twice.
No.
I would write it in the agreement as, like, both phases as one property.
Okay.
So, there'd be two… so, for example, the property I'm talking about is Lakeside.
There'd be two Lakeside listings.
There would be 2 lakeside listings.
But…
We would bill you as one property for those.
Okay.
Could I see what it looks like from a user's point of view?
Like… if they end up on rentable, like, what they see, like, how are they searching by Charlottesville, like, how the listings look…
Yeah, let me go to that page… Yeah, so, like, if I was to go to Rentable.
co.
This is how it initially pops up, so if we go Charlotte's…
Charlottesville, Virginia, you would click search, and it would pull up all the properties, pictures.
details about it.
Okay, so we're already on there.
Is this real?
Did you see a lakeside Apartments?
So we're Carriage Hill, Lakeside, and the Reserve at Belvedere.
Yes, so here's what's going on.
So, we integrate with Appfolio, and Appfolio has, like, a marketing distribution where they Send out, like, a basic listing, essentially, to…
Yeah.
For all your properties.
The issue with that is we cap your leads at 5.

And then you don't get our syndicate network, you don't get our social boost, you don't get our AI tool, so you're getting, like, the bare, basic minimum of… for all of those sites that they syndicate to.

With ours, when you have a partnership with us, we don't… cap your leads, you get everything that we've talked about on the call, and then when we syndicate out, we actually syndicate out as more of a middle-upper tier, so we would rank higher for those sites than what Appfolio does.
So, nobody else in Charlottesville is on rentable?
We're listed first.
Good question.
It says we have 175 properties.
Yours may be, like, some of the more engaged with.
You said yours is Carriage Hill, Lakeside, and Belvedere?
Yeah.
There's Lakeside again.
Yeah, which I'm assuming that's because it's Phase 1, Phase 2.
Let's see here… Wood Lane's not yours, right?
No.
Okay, so, let's see…
If I just click into yours, what it does is it shows all your pictures, you can click in and see more, it shows the price ranges, it shows the bed… Beds and floor plans, and the price range is there.
You can see a street view, and a map view, and a satellite view.
It pops in amenities, which I'm sure you have more amenities than just these three, but that's all we're getting from Matfolio.
Pulls in, like, what a security deposit would be, and gives, like, a little property description.
Is this your actual… I don't know if that's your actual property phone number.
No, it's not.
Yeah.
Which, that may just be because that's the information within AppFolio, or maybe it's a Twilio number, I'm not…
Yeah, it could be… I think it might be a tracking number.
Yeah.
But I don't know what the extension is.
I… yeah, I don't know either.
So… so all that information would be updated,
Yeah.
And then you would have, like, your company logo added in here.
Saying it was a, cathcart Group.
property.
Let's see here, though…
That's the telegun, yeah.
Yeah… Is Abington crossing yours?
No.
Okay.
Which, this may be a same… same type thing listed from, yeah, updated over a month ago, so… This one was syndicated and then… or just, like, manually built by somebody.
So… So yeah, this is what your listing would look like, though.
Okay.
Yeah.
That's why it's on there, though, is because of your appfolio.
Yeah, makes sense.
Yup.
So then, how are the, like, social ads built based off of these same pictures and information?
Yes, so all the social ads are gonna be built off of the information from AppFolio, and, like, what we pull into our site.

So pictures will be listed, details about it will be listed, and that AI tool that I mentioned is also working there to make sure that we're optimizing that social ad, like, to the best of its ability.

So, if it's getting more attraction from this pool and clubhouse picture, it'll move it first, but if it's more attraction to, like, the picture of the garage here, or, like, the kitchen, then whatever is getting clicked on more often will move that towards the front of that social ad.
Okay, got it.
So then what… is it a 12-month contract, or what are we locked into?
Yeah, we normally do annual agreements, but right now we're doing as short as 6 months.
Okay, and is there any sort of, like, tryout option?
6 months.
Okay.
And then…
I'm saying for $1.
99 a month is through 2026, at least.
Yes.
Okay.
And with our agreement, because our price is only $199, we normally require a 5 property minimum.
Since you only have 8 properties total, though, I would be able to get it low, like, 3.
3 properties for a trial.
So that would get you at least all your Charlottesville properties.
Right.
Or it would give you 3 different areas, whichever you'd prefer.
Yeah.
Okay, that sounds good.
Perfect.
When.
Send me a list of all of the, like, other sites that you all syndicate to.
Yeah, yeah, I can send that.
I can also send a follow-up with all the information that we've talked about as well, with pricing and…
Perfect.
I'll be back in everything.
Sounds good.
Cool.
When… when would you all be looking to make a decision on adding an additional partner?
Probably within the next… Probably by the end of October.
Okay.
My budget's due at the end of October, so I have to have it in there by then.
So… There's a lot of people on vacation.
This week and next, so… probably gonna really start finalizing things, like, towards the end of September, and then… continue conversations in October.
That's fine.
That is perfectly fine.
And just so you're aware, I can delay your billing as well, so, like, let's say you made the decision mid-September that you wanted to move forward with everything.
I can delay your billing until… November 1st.
Okay.
Got it.
Yeah, I mean, I don't know… I guess maybe we could start the trial with 3 properties, potentially, this year, but… I mean, we didn't budget for it for 2025, so…
You'd be looking to start it for 2026.
Probably, but since it is pretty affordable, like, we might be able to start the trial period with 3 of the properties.
Okay.
this year… It might honestly depend on traffic and if we need to… Start pulling out all the stops.
Gotcha.
Alright, let's see… Cool, okay.
Well, that works.
I can get you over all that information, Anne, and, is there a good day in the upcoming weeks for us to follow up with one another?
Let me…
Review what you send over, and then get with my boss once she's back from vacation.
And kind of get all my ducks in a row with all of my other budget items, and then I will… I will get back with you if that works.
Yeah, no, that works.
I just wanted to make sure it wasn't, you know, I didn't over-reach out or anything.
I didn't know if, like, we could schedule something for, like, maybe the 19th.
Yeah, if you don't hear from me by the end of September, reach out.
And I'll definitely reach out to you if I have more questions.
Okay.
And then… If it's something we decide to move forward with, I'll probably need to do another call similar to this with more people on it.
From my team.
That's fine.
I will make a note there, and we can touch base before the end of September.
Sounds good!
Thank you, Nathan.
Thank you, Anne, it was great meeting you, and I'll get that info over here to you shortly.
Sounds good, have a good day.
You as well.
